en.extract:
The São Tomé prinia (Prinia molleri) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae. It is endemic to São Tomé and Príncipe and is found in the island of São Tomé. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist montane forest and subtropical or tropical moist shrubland. It is found primarily in open habitats and at the edges of forests.
The species was named by José Vicente Barbosa du Bocage in 1887.
It frequently performs short aerial displays.


Diet
It is primarily insectivorous, but also sometimes eats plants, including Erythrina flowers. It is suspected to forage in rotting tree trunks.

[[File:Prinia molleri MHNT.jpg|thumb|eggs - [[MHNT]]]]
The '''São Tomé prinia''' ('''''Prinia molleri''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Cisticolidae]].<ref>{{Avibase|843FF0829400704A}}</ref> It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[São Tomé and Príncipe]] and is found in the island of [[São Tomé Island|São Tomé]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" /> Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]] and [[subtropical or tropical moist shrubland]]. It is found primarily in open habitats and at the edges of forests.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last=Lo Cascio |first=Pietro |date=2021-10-04 |title=Ornithological observations from São Tomé and Príncipe  islands (Gulf of Guinea, West Africa) |journal=Biodiversity Journal |volume=12 |issue=4 |pages=765–775 |doi=10.31396/biodiv.jour.2021.12.4.765.775 |issn=2039-0394|doi-access=free }}</ref>

The species was named by [[José Vicente Barbosa du Bocage]] in 1887.

It frequently performs short aerial displays.<ref name=":0" />

== Diet ==
It is primarily insectivorous, but also sometimes eats plants, including ''[[Erythrina]]'' flowers.<ref name=":0" /> It is suspected to forage in rotting tree trunks.<ref name=":0" />
